meta.description: Senior-dev baseline answer cases — factual lookup, temporal/structural, refusal. Three of the ten baseline cases planned for v0.4.0; remaining seven ship in a fixtures follow-up PR. Retrieved context is inlined; PR 5 orchestrator will produce context via live retrieval against the frozen seed brain.

cases.query: What database did I pick for the pbrain data pipeline and why?
required_answer_facts: Reason was hybrid search — pgvector plus full Postgres in one system

retrieved_context.text: ADR 001: database choice for pbrain. Decision: Postgres with pgvector over SQLite and DuckDB. Date: 2026-02-14. Primary reason: pgvector gives HNSW vector search plus real Postgres features (triggers, JSONB, full-text, row-level security) in one system. PGLite is the zero-config default for solo users; managed Supabase is the scale path via `pbrain migrate --to supabase`. SQLite was considered but lacks first-class vector search. DuckDB was ruled out because it's optimized for analytics, not OLTP.

cases.query: When was the Attention Is All You Need paper published and who are two of the authors?
required_answer_facts: The paper was published in 2017 at NeurIPS
Vaswani is one of the authors
Shazeer is one of the authors
forbidden_answer_facts: The paper uses RoPE positional embeddings

retrieved_context.text: Reading notes on Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017). The paper replaces both recurrence and convolutions with pure attention. Scaled dot-product attention divides Q·Kᵀ by √d_k before softmax. Multi-head (h=8) outperforms single-head by roughly 0.9 BLEU. Published at NeurIPS in June 2017. Listed authors include Ashish Vaswani and Noam Shazeer. Position encoding is sinusoidal.

cases.query: When did I ship version 9.0 of project-fictional-z and what was in the release?
forbidden_answer_facts: project-fictional-z shipped version 9.0 on any specific date

retrieved_context.text: ADR 001: database choice. Postgres + pgvector. No mention of any project-fictional-z or version 9.0.
Reading notes on the 2017 Transformer paper. Nothing about project-fictional-z or any 9.0 release.
